channel.title: Bali Economy on Bali Kini
channel.description: Recent content in Bali Economy on Bali Kini

item.title: Bali's Hotel & Restaurant Tax Hits Rp2.9 Trillion Despite 7% Tourist Drop
item.description:
Despite a notable decline in foreign tourist arrivals, Bali’s tourism revenue is painting a surprisingly optimistic picture. The province’s Hotel and Restaurant Tax (PHR) through the end of May 2026 reached Rp2.9 trillion — a jump of approximately Rp300 billion compared to the same period in 2025, when the figure stood at Rp2.62 trillion.

PHR Figures: January–May 2026

Bali Governor Wayan Koster revealed the data at several tourism forums in late May and early June 2026. The official provincial figures show:

item.title: US Companies Deepen Investment in Bali: Marriott to PepsiCo
item.description:
Several major US corporations have officially reaffirmed their commitment to expand and deepen their investments in Bali. The announcements came at an Executive Briefing titled “Impact of Innovation on Bali’s Economic Development: A Showcase of U.S. Companies’ Initiatives”, held in Bali on Thursday, June 4, 2026.

The event was organized by the US-ASEAN Business Council (USABC), which spotlighted the various initiatives of American companies contributing to Bali’s economic development amid global economic uncertainty.
